NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, They labor in vain who build it; Unless the LORD guards the city, The watchman stays awake in vain.
NKJV.2: It is vain for you to rise up early, To sit up late, To eat the bread of sorrows; For so He gives His beloved sleep.
NKJV.3: Behold, children are a heritage from the LORD, The fruit of the womb is a reward.
NKJV.4: Like arrows in the hand of a warrior, So are the children of one’s youth.
NKJV.5: Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them; They shall not be ashamed, But shall speak with their enemies in the gate.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S> for Solomon<S>8010</S>. Except the LORD<S>3068</S> build<S>1129</S> the house<S>1004</S>, they labour<S>5998</S> in vain<S>7723</S> that build<S>1129</S> it: except the LORD<S>3068</S> keep<S>8104</S> the city<S>5892</S>, the watchman<S>8104</S> waketh<S>8245</S> but in vain<S>7723</S>. <sup>for: or, of Solomon</sup> <sup>that: Heb. that are builders of it in it</sup>
KJV.2: It is vain<S>7723</S> for you to rise up<S>6965</S> early<S>7925</S>, to sit up<S>3427</S> late<S>309</S>, to eat<S>398</S> the bread<S>3899</S> of sorrows<S>6089</S>: for so he giveth<S>5414</S> his beloved<S>3039</S> sleep<S>8142</S>.
KJV.3: Lo, children<S>1121</S> are an heritage<S>5159</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>: and the fruit<S>6529</S> of the womb<S>990</S> is his reward<S>7939</S>.

ESV.1: Unless the LORD builds the house, those who build it labor in vain. Unless the LORD watches over the city, the watchman stays awake in vain.
ESV.2: It is in vain that you rise up early and go late to rest, eating the bread of anxious toil; for he gives to his beloved sleep.
ESV.3: Behold, children are a heritage from the LORD, the fruit of the womb a reward.
ESV.4: Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are the children  of one's youth.
ESV.5: Blessed is the man who fills his quiver with them! He shall not be put to shame when he speaks with his enemies in the gate.

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents, of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, They labor in vain who build it; Unless the LORD guards the city, The watchman keeps awake in vain.
NASB.2: It is vain for you to rise up early, To retire late, To eat the bread of painful labors; For He gives to His beloved [even in his] sleep.
NASB.3: Behold, children are a gift of the LORD, The fruit of the womb is a reward.
NASB.4: Like arrows in the hand of a warrior, So are the children of one's youth.
NASB.5: How blessed is the man whose quiver is full of them; They will not be ashamed When they speak with their enemies in the gate.

NLT.1:
Unless the LORD builds a house,
the work of the builders is wasted.
Unless the LORD protects a city,
guarding it with sentries will do no good.
NLT.2:
It is useless for you to work so hard
from early morning until late at night,
anxiously working for food to eat;
for God gives rest to his loved ones.
NLT.3:
Children are a gift from the LORD;
they are a reward from him.
NLT.4:
Children born to a young man
are like arrows in a warrior’s hands.
NLT.5:
How joyful is the man whose quiver is full of them!
He will not be put to shame when he confronts his accusers at the city gates.

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. By Solomon. Unless Yahweh builds the house, they labor in vain who build it. Unless Yahweh watches over the city, the watchman guards it in vain.
WEB.2: It is vain for you to rise up early, to stay up late, eating the bread of toil; for he gives sleep to his loved ones.
WEB.3: Behold, children are a heritage of Yahweh. The fruit of the womb is his reward.
WEB.4: As arrows in the hand of a mighty man, so are the children of youth.
WEB.5: Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them. They won’t be disappointed when they speak with their enemies in the gate.

CSB17.1:
A song of ascents. Of Solomon. [1] Unless the LORD builds a house,
its builders labor over it in vain; ⓔ
unless the LORD watches over a city,
the watchman stays alert in vain. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
In vain you get up early and stay up late,
working hard to have enough food — 
yes, he gives sleep to the one he loves. [1] ⓖ
CSB17.3:
Sons are indeed a heritage from the LORD,
offspring, a reward. ⓗ
CSB17.4:
Like arrows in the hand of a warrior
are the sons born in one’s youth. ⓘ
CSB17.5:
Happy is the man who has filled his quiver with them. ⓙ
They will never be put to shame
when they speak with their enemies at the city gate. ⓚ

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house,
They labor in vain who build it;
Unless the LORD guards the city,
The watchman keeps awake in vain.
AMP.2:
It is vain for you to rise early,
To retire late,
To eat the bread of anxious labors—
For He gives [blessings] to His beloved even in his sleep.
AMP.3:
Behold, children are a heritage and gift from the LORD,
The fruit of the womb a reward.
AMP.4:
Like arrows in the hand of a warrior,
So are the children of one's youth.
AMP.5:
How blessed [happy and fortunate] is the man whose quiver is filled with them;
They will not be ashamed
When they speak with their enemies [in gatherings] at the [city] gate.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song of Solomon
If God doesn't build the house,
the builders only build shacks.
If God doesn't guard the city,
the night watchman might as well nap.
MSG.2:
It's useless to rise early and go to bed late,
and work your worried fingers to the bone.
Don't you know he enjoys
giving rest to those he loves?
MSG.3:
Don't you see that children are God's best gift?
the fruit of the womb his generous legacy?
MSG.4:
Like a warrior's fistful of arrows
are the children of a vigorous youth.
MSG.5:
Oh, how blessed are you parents,
with your quivers full of children!
Your enemies don't stand a chance against you;
you'll sweep them right off your doorstep.

NIV.1:
Psalm 127
A song of ascents. Of Solomon.
Unless the Lord builds the house,
its builders labor in vain.
Unless the Lord watches over the city,
the watchmen stand guard in vain.
NIV.2:
In vain you rise early
and stay up late,
toiling for food to eat—
for he grants sleep to those he loves.
NIV.3:
Sons are a heritage from the Lord,
children a reward from him.
NIV.4:
Like arrows in the hands of a warrior
are sons born in one’s youth.
NIV.5:
Blessed is the man
whose quiver is full of them.
They will not be put to shame
when they contend with their enemies in the gate.

NRSVCE.1:
Unless the LORD builds the house,
those who build it labor in vain.
Unless the LORD guards the city,
the guard keeps watch in vain.
NRSVCE.2:
It is in vain that you rise up early
and go late to rest,
eating the bread of anxious toil;
for he gives sleep to his beloved.
NRSVCE.3:
Sons are indeed a heritage from the LORD,
the fruit of the womb a reward.
NRSVCE.4:
Like arrows in the hand of a warrior
are the sons of one’s youth.
NRSVCE.5:
Happy is the man who has
his quiver full of them.
He shall not be put to shame
when he speaks with his enemies in the gate.

NET.1: If the LORD does not build a house, then those who build it work in vain. If the LORD does not guard a city, then the watchman stands guard in vain.
NET.2: It is vain for you to rise early, come home late, and work so hard for your food. Yes, he can provide for those whom he loves even when they sleep.
NET.3: Yes, sons are a gift from the LORD, the fruit of the womb is a reward.
NET.4: Sons born during one’s youth are like arrows in a warrior’s hand.
NET.5: How blessed is the man who fills his quiver with them! They will not be put to shame when they confront enemies at the city gate.
